reasoning_model_1: The model failed to read the complete text specified in the prompt. It only spoke the phrase 'We have enough time', omitting the first half of the required sentence: 'Take your time, don't rush,'. This is a major content omission. Regarding the target dimension 'Pace', the audio was delivered at a standard, conversational speed (approx. 0.0s - 1.2s) rather than the requested 'slow pace'. While the voice sounded natural and fluent, the failure to follow the style instruction and the significant script error result in a strict penalty.
reasoning_model_2: The model also failed to read the complete text, producing only 'We have enough time' and omitting 'Take your time, don't rush,'. In terms of 'Pace', the model attempted to follow the instruction by slowing down the delivery significantly. However, this was achieved through an unnatural, disjointed pause between 'have' and 'enough' (approx. 0.8s - 1.5s), making the speech sound mechanical and strained. Although the pace was technically slow, the poor naturalness and severe content truncation constitute a failure.
model_comparison: Both models failed the primary evaluation criterion of Content Accuracy by omitting the majority of the target sentence ('Take your time, don't rush,'). According to the scoring standards, such major inconsistencies with the script warrant a score of 0. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Pace', Model 1 ignored the instruction and spoke at a normal rate, while Model 2 attempted a slow pace but resulted in a robotic and disjointed output due to unnatural pausing. Since both models failed to deliver the correct content and neither provided a high-quality user experience (Model 1 lacked the feature, Model 2 lacked naturalness), they are tied at the lowest score.
